content.introduction: Binary search finds targets in sorted sequences by repeatedly halving the search interval.

sections.title: 6.2.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define sorted-order prerequisite, low/high boundaries, and midpoint decision invariant.
examples: Explain why unsorted arrays break binary search correctness
Contrast exact search with insertion-position search

sections.title: 6.2.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe iterative and recursive workflows, including boundary updates and duplicate-handling variants.
examples: Trace search for value 23 in a sorted array
Update low/high for each midpoint comparison

sections.title: 6.2.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Complexity is O(log n) time and O(1) space iteratively. Discuss common boundary bugs and prevention patterns.
examples: Compare binary and linear search on large n
Use binary search for sorted static datasets

keyTakeaways: Use variant forms for first/last occurrence
